chief_complaint: A term male neonate presented at birth with severe respiratory failure.
history: Multisystem inflammatory syndrome in neonates (MIS-N) is a rare condition linked to perinatal exposure to SARS-CoV-2. The infant developed diffuse skin peeling, acute kidney injury, and hypertension over the next few days. The mother was RT-PCR positive for SARS-CoV-2, and the neonate had markedly elevated SARS-CoV-2 IgG with negative RT-PCR.
exam: Vital signs and physical examination findings were not explicitly stated.
labs: Echocardiography showed elevated pulmonary pressures without structural abnormalities. Serial echocardiography revealed diffuse endomyocardial and coronary intimal echogenicity suggestive of calcification. The infant was RT-PCR negative for SARS-CoV-2, but had markedly elevated SARS-CoV-2 IgG.
correct_diagnosis: Multisystem inflammatory syndrome of newborn (MIS-N) with endomyocardial calcification and systemic hypertension
differentials: Multisystem inflammatory syndrome of newborn (MIS-N) with endomyocardial calcification and systemic hypertension
Persistent pulmonary hypertension of the newborn
correct_ranking: Multisystem inflammatory syndrome of newborn (MIS-N) with endomyocardial calcification and systemic hypertension
Persistent pulmonary hypertension of the newborn
